title: potentilla arenaria var. subcaulis
intro: Potentilla arenaria var. subcaulis, also known as dwarf or alpine cinquefoil, is a low-growing perennial plant belonging to the Rosaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Potentilla arenaria var. subcaulis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
